inline_comments.message: This should be latest version, i.e. 1.0.5-latest
A similar change could be made in sdnc.cluster, removing the need to explicitly set env vars HOME or OOM_HOME
I have made a note in SDNC-342 that this reference to this password (for the jolokia service) needs to be made configurable.
This file isn't configured by OOM, so it ideally shouldn't be installed this way.
Files like this that aren't configured by OOM shouldn't really be in OOM.

Also, let's call it sdnc-prom to make it clear where it originates from.

If the chart is in sdnc/charts, then it is always deployed, even in single-site deployments.

Please prefix with ONAP. I think this is the right place to put the full name (what does PROM stand for?) instead of acronym.

While testing, I noticed the chart is not prefixed with sdnc-. This makes it difficult to understand what it is related to and to see all sdnc components status together while running "kubectl get pod". Would you like to rename the chart (and the directory) to sdnc-prom?
better use {{include "common.fillname" . }}-configmap here and in the deployment.yaml
I think there is a helm value field for this.  if this file is part of the chart then please make use of the appropriate value fields

From the readme https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=music/prom.git;a=blob_plain;f=README.md;hb=HEAD

PROM = Policy Driven Ownership Management
I agree that this could be named sdnc-prom.  In the future, prom may also be used for APP-C site redundancy, and hence an appc-prom pod.

This file is not part of the chart.  All geo/bin scripts are simply CLIs used to manage the cluster - maybe they should be moved out of the sdnc/resources folder to make that more obvious.

That said, the password could be parsed from values.yaml to prevent hard-coding here - sdnc.isPrimaryCluster script does something similar with isPrimaryCluster value.

That's a good idea.  Either way, wherever it ends up its path needs to be added to the .helmignore file otherwise helm will still upload them to the remote tiller even though it isn't used in the chart.
Done

Had to change directory name to sdnc-prom
sending a new patch set soon. Sorry for missing this.

I think there is something I do not understand.
sdnc-prom chart should be located in sdnc-prom directory. This is how helm works, according to helm documentation.
https://docs.helm.sh/developing_charts/#charts
I think prom directory is redundant.
You should use {{ include "common.fullname" . }}, same as defined in the config-map.
same here
This should not be hard-coded. Please look at other charts how this should be done.

service name should be sndc-prom.
However I do not see any service.yaml under template directory. Does this component not have a service?
In that case service section in values.yaml is also redundant.

You are right Borislov. 
Fixed it.
Done
Done
prom does not have service. removing this section.
Done

Can this be changed to sdnc.prom.geoEnabled or sdnc.geoEnabled or something that would make it clear when I add it to helm command? Currently it is not self-explanatory:
helm upgrade -i aaa local/onap --namespace onap --set global.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent -f ~/global-values.yaml  --set config.geoEnabled=true
Also, what when there will be appc geo-redundancy?

This results in creation of the coredns directory under /dockerdata-nfs, while we would like it to be created under the Helm release dir.
Example: my helm release is named aaa. all files are under /dockerdata-nfs/aaa, except coredns:
root@borislav-rancher:~/onap/master/oom/kubernetes# ls /dockerdata-nfs/*/
/dockerdata-nfs/aaa/:
aaf  aai  appc  dbc  dcae  log  mariadb  message-router  mso  nbi  nexus  oof  portal  redis  robot  sdc  sdnc  sms

/dockerdata-nfs/coredns/:

Look how this is done in other charts:
path: {{ .Values.global.persistence.mountPath | default .Values.persistence.mountPath }}/{{ .Release.Name }}/{{ .Values.persistence.mountSubPath }}

Also, it is better to use Persistent Volume and not hostPath.

Done. 
sorry... I don't know when the appc Geo-Redundancy will be ready.  I will ask Product owner and will let you know.

Done.
I did not get your last statement " It is better to use Persistent Volume and not hostPath". I noticed most of the other Charts used hostPath. 

Please see the new patch set and let me know if that is OK.

Sorry, just noticed this.
This will not work if consul and sdnc are deployed in separate helm releases.
It also creates an unnecessary dependency between consul and sdnc.
IF you need this information in consul, better add the configmap in consul, not in sdnc. There maybe deployments with consul and without sdnc.
Please replace PROM here with "Policy Driven Ownership Management"

Release.Name should not be used here.
I see you share this configMap with consul. Better create it in consul.
Consul and sndc can be in different helm releases, causing this not to work.

You are right. 
The configmap is also used by prom. So kept it.

Created env var inside consul (only 2 were used in consul.. so created only 2 env var
will fix this later.

I understand your valid concern. 

sdnc.prom.geoEnabled means prom is GEO. this is not right.

geoEnabled is all about sdnc and if set to true, prom will be installed. I can not use sdnc.prom.geoEnabled. 

To change config.geoEnabled to sdnc.config.geoEnabled, I will have to create "sdnc" (in values.yaml) and move config.xxx to under sdnc.config.xxx.
It will impact 15 more variables and wherever they are referred needs to be changed.

Would it be possible to keep this, at it is for now and open a JIRA in ONAP to address your request?
Done

Tested.. you are right. consul without sdnc faces problem! 

kept the configmap as prom is using it. 
Created env var inside consul (only 2 were used in consul.. so created only 2 env var)
Not sure why this is needed. If not needed, please remove in a separate commit.
